name: Customer support copilot suite
description: Seven specialist agents that coach a live support AGENT in real time. Demonstrates turn-based, keyword, silence, and event (Phase-2) triggers; high-priority escalation alerting; KB suggestion; a sentiment variable tracker; an SLA/idle monitor on a silence tick; cooldown gating; and one-hop question->answer event coordination.

agents.name: Conversation Phase Tracker
agents.text: You silently track which phase of the support conversation we are in.
agents._doc: Silent state agent. Writes the `phase` variable every turn (no whisper). Watch the Blackboard panel: `phase` flips greeting -> troubleshooting -> escalation -> resolution. It has cooldown 0 so it can re-write every single turn.

agents.name: Sentiment Tracker
agents.text: You silently track the customer's sentiment.
agents._doc: Silent variable tracker. Writes the `sentiment` variable (negative / neutral / positive) every turn it matches, with NO whisper. Watch the `sentiment` variable in the Blackboard panel flip as the customer's mood changes. Rules are ordered worst-first so a single angry line wins over a polite one in the same window. Teaching point: it reclassifies from the LAST rendered line only -- 'cancel the whole subscription' (no explicit anger word) reads as neutral.

agents.name: Escalation Alert
agents.text: Detect anger, refund, or cancellation language and alert the support agent to de-escalate.
agents._doc: Highest priority (10). Fires a WARNING on anger / refund / cancel / escalation language and records an `escalation_risk` fact + increments escalation_count in private memory. Cooldown is 12s ON PURPOSE: when two escalation lines land <12s apart, the SECOND turn is gated by cooldown -- look for it under the turn's 'skipped: cooldown' list. After 12s elapse it can fire again (cooldown recovery on the final turn).

speak.content: Escalation risk. Acknowledge the frustration first, do NOT defend policy yet. Empathize, then offer one concrete next step.

facts.value: customer used anger / refund / cancel language

agents.name: Knowledge Base Suggester
agents.text: When the customer describes a known product problem, suggest the relevant knowledge-base article.
agents._doc: Turn-based SUGGESTION agent. Fires when the customer describes a recognizable product issue (login, sync, password, billing) and surfaces the matching KB article. Cooldown 4s: two issue descriptions in quick succession can gate the second -- but mostly it fires, and is the 'helpful answer' counterpart to the angry-customer agents.

speak.content: Known issue. Suggest KB article 'Resolving login & sync errors' and walk them through the reset steps before anything else.

agents.name: Question Detector
agents.text: Detect when the customer asks a question and signal other agents.
agents._doc: Emits the `customer_question` EVENT and queues the question. The event wakes the Answer Suggester in Phase 2 of the SAME turn -- this is the framework's one-hop coordination. Silent itself (no whisper); watch the Events row and the `pending_questions` queue grow.

agents.name: Answer Suggester
agents.text: When a customer question is detected, suggest how to answer it.
agents._doc: EVENT-triggered. Runs ONLY in Phase 2, and ONLY on turns where Question Detector emitted `customer_question`. On every other turn it appears under 'skipped (trigger_type_mismatch)'. This is the clearest view of Phase-1 -> Phase-2 coordination and the extra LLM round-trip it costs (see the per-phase latency).

speak.content: Customer asked a direct question. Answer it plainly first -- give a clear yes/no or timeframe -- then add the detail. Don't bury the answer.

agents.name: SLA Idle Monitor
agents.text: Warn the support agent when the customer has gone quiet and the SLA response window is at risk.
agents._doc: SILENCE-triggered. It does NOT run on normal talking turns (it has only TriggerType.SILENCE, so on text turns it shows under 'skipped: trigger_type_mismatch'). It fires only on a turn the host marks trigger=silence. Its rule has an empty `when` so it always speaks on that tick -- a deliberate contrast with the sales demo, where NO silence agent existed and the dead-air turn produced nothing.

speak.content: Customer has gone quiet and the SLA reply window is closing. Send a proactive check-in: 'Still here with you -- are you able to try that step?'
